data.content:
The issue is classic F# — **file compilation order matters**. `Services.fs` is listed before `Domain.fs` in the `.fsproj`, but it depends on types defined in `Domain.fs`. F# requires dependencies to appear earlier in the file list.

Fixing by swapping the order:
data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. In F#, file order matters — files can only reference modules/types defined in files listed before them in the `.fsproj`. 

Currently the order is:
1. `Services.fs` — depends on `Domain.fs` (uses `Order`, `OrderId` types)
2. `Domain.fs` — defines `Order`, `OrderId`, `OrderItem`
3. `Program.fs` — depends on both

`Services.fs` is listed before `Domain.fs`, but `Services.fs` opens `BrokenOrder.Domain`, which is defined in `Domain.fs`. So `Domain.fs` must come before `Services.fs`.


I need to reorder the files so `Domain.fs` comes first, then `Services.fs`, and finally `Program.fs`.

diff --git a/tmp/sv-084719a7403c4d159dc6034ac774bc13/fsharp/BrokenOrder/BrokenOrder.fsproj b/tmp/sv-084719a7403c4d159dc6034ac774bc13/fsharp/BrokenOrder/BrokenOrder.fsproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-084719a7403c4d159dc6034ac774bc13/fsharp/BrokenOrder/BrokenOrder.fsproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-084719a7403c4d159dc6034ac774bc13/fsharp/BrokenOrder/BrokenOrder.fsproj
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
-    <Compile Include="Services.fs" />
     <Compile Include="Domain.fs" />
+    <Compile Include="Services.fs" />
     <Compile Include="Program.fs" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content: Build succeeds. The fix was reordering the `<Compile>` items in `BrokenOrder.fsproj` so `Domain.fs` comes before `Services.fs`. In F#, files are compiled top-to-bottom as listed — a file can only reference types/modules from files listed **above** it.
